what_it_does: Records metadata for an image attachment linked to a design-source channel entry.
observations: The file consists only of YAML frontmatter with no body content.
The target is a custom URI 'image://design-source/vt-property/p-1'.
It includes an added timestamp in ISO 8601 format.
standouts: The target uses a custom 'image://' URI scheme pointing to 'design-source/vt-property/p-1'.
The file acts as a pointer/reference rather than containing the image payload.
conventions: Follows a channel-attachment ledger format with id, channel, attachment_type, target, and added fields.
Uses YAML frontmatter for structured metadata.
References external assets via a project-specific URI scheme.
concerns: The 'image://' URI is opaque and not resolvable from this file alone.
There is no checksum, MIME type, dimensions, or file size for the referenced image.
No alt text, caption, or description of the image is provided.
The owning channel entry or relation semantics are not explicit.
notes: A lightweight ledger stub that points a design-source channel attachment record to an external image asset at vt-property/p-1.
questions: Which specific design-source entry does this attachment belong to?
What does the image at 'vt-property/p-1' depict?
How is the 'image://' URI resolved to an actual file or resource?
Is 'p-1' a versioned or stable identifier?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to record an image attachment reference, and the content does exactly that.
intention_signals: It appears designed to catalog image attachments for design-source channel entries, keeping attachment metadata separate from the binary asset payload.
contribution: Provides a stable, timestamped pointer that links a channel attachment record to an external image asset, supporting asset tracking without embedding binary content.
summary_for_embedding: Markdown file with YAML frontmatter recording an image attachment (att-7c304dc4) in the design-source channel targeting image://design-source/vt-property/p-1, added 2026-06-22.
intention_for_embedding: To register an image attachment reference for a design-source channel entry.
